action.reasoning: Observation: Best result was 90.5 safe_follow_steps (iter 14) with PD control. Current predict.py is truncated. Main bottleneck: robot spends too much time far from human (avg_dist=4.2m) due to aggressive angular gating and getting stuck on obstacles. Analysis: 1) Angular gating too aggressive during approach (caps forward speed at large angles), 2) No obstacle avoidance causes stuckness, 3) Stuck recovery too slow. Decision: Write complete predict.py with: less aggressive angular gating when far, gentle depth-based obstacle avoidance when far+humanoid not detected, faster stuck recovery, keep iter 14 PD gains.
action.command: python train.py && python -c "import py_compile; py_compile.compile('predict.py', doraise=True); print('Syntax OK')"
